title: 中考六选五 1: The Problem with Easy Answers
passage:
When students face a difficult question, many of them want a quick answer. They may ask a classmate, search online or use an AI tool. Getting help is not wrong. 1. ______ If students only copy the answer, they miss the chance to think.

A difficult question is valuable because it forces students to slow down. They need to read carefully, compare ideas and test possible answers. 2. ______ This process may feel uncomfortable, but it builds real understanding.

However, some students mistake “getting the answer” for “learning the skill”. These two things are not the same. 3. ______ A student may know the final answer but still be unable to solve a similar question next time.

Teachers can help by asking students to explain their thinking. Instead of only checking whether an answer is correct, teachers can ask, “How did you get it?” 4. ______ In this way, students learn to value the thinking process.

For students, the best habit is to use help wisely. They can look at hints, compare methods or ask for explanations. 5. ______ The goal is not to avoid difficulty, but to learn from it.

questions.label: Questions 1-5 - Sentence Insertion
questions.instruction: Choose the best sentence from A-F for each blank in the passage. There is one extra sentence you do not need.

choices.text: But the way they use help matters more than the help itself.
A correct answer without understanding is often a weak kind of success.
These questions push students to show the steps behind their answers.
They should still try to solve the problem before accepting someone else’s answer.
In fact, the struggle is often where learning really happens.
Students can improve faster if they collect more answers from different sources.
